title: Enthusiast recreates 43-year-old Apple Lisa with FPGA board first commercial computer with a GUI faithfully cloned with modernized machine
summary: An enthusiast recreated the Apple Lisa with an FPGA board, faithfully cloning its GUI and hardware. The Apple Lisa was the first commercial computer with a graphical user interface. This modernized machine is a testament to retro technology innovation.
insight: Reviving vintage tech showcases innovation's enduring legacy and potential.

title: New server-focused SPEC CPU 2026 benchmarking suite has results for a Raspberry Pi 5 updated tools feature more tests and can run a wide range of systems
summary: The SPEC CPU 2026 is an updated benchmarking suite featuring more tests and emphasizing portability, allowing it to run on a wide range of systems, from high-end servers to low-end devices like the Raspberry Pi 5.
insight: Industry-standard benchmarking now spans entire tech ecosystems efficiently.

title: Intel swipes Qualcomm veteran of 25 years to lead client computing Alex Katouzian jumps ship to oversee consumer CPUs and physical AI
summary: Intel appoints Alex Katouzian to lead client computing and AI efforts, signaling a shift towards AI-driven and edge computing systems. The move also confirms Pushkar Ranade as CTO. Katouzian brings 25 years of experience from Qualcomm.
insight: Intel enhances AI capabilities with Qualcomm veteran's expertise leadership

title: Trump administration considers mandatory pre-release vetting of AI models Anthropic's Mythos cited as catalyst for policy reversal
summary: The Trump administration is considering an executive order for AI model review. This would involve a government review process for new AI models before public release. The goal is to ensure AI safety and accountability.
insight: Regulating AI releases could prevent catastrophic AI misuse scenarios

title: Steam Controller scalpers are asking for $300+ on eBay Valve's $99 controller demands high price as pre-orders sell out almost immediately
summary: Valve's Steam Controller sold out quickly, prompting scalpers to demand $300+ on eBay.
insight: Gamers face price gouging due to high demand and limited supply.

title: Get a DDR5 starter build with a Ryzen 5 7600X and ASRock B650M Pro motherboard for 23% off $430 bundle helps avoid inflated RAM prices
summary: Get a DDR5 starter build with a Ryzen 5 7600X and ASRock B650M Pro motherboard for a discounted price of $329.10, helping to avoid inflated RAM prices.
insight: Avoid expensive RAM prices with a bundled motherboard combo.

title: Secretlab launches Mandalorian Titan Evo gaming chair in Star Wars collection for May the 4th collab builds with a new high-end themed chair
summary: Secretlab releases Mandalorian Titan Evo gaming chair for Star Wars fans on May 4th, as part of a new collection, combining comfort and high-end gaming with Star Wars-themed designs.
insight: Gaming brands collaborate with popular franchises to boost brand appeal.

title: Alienware 16 Area-51 review: OLED screen update
summary: The Alienware 16 Area-51 features a powerful performance, OLED display option, and updated design.
insight: OLED display upgrade offers superior visuals and gaming experience.

title: AMD Ryzen AI 5 435G APU breaks cover in early benchmarks six-core Zen 5 chip goes head-to-head with the Ryzen 5 8600G for budget PC builders
summary: The Ryzen AI 5 435G APU rivals the Ryzen 5 8600G in performance, according to early Geekbench 6 benchmarks. This new chip, featuring a six-core Zen 5 design, may offer budget PC builders a compelling alternative. The AMD Ryzen AI 5 435G appears to offer comparable performance to existing options.
insight: Budget builders have a competitive six-core APU option emerging.

title: CISA flags actively exploited Copy Fail Linux kernel flaw enabling root takeover across major distros unpatched systems may remain vulnerable to attack
summary: CISA warns of actively exploited Copy Fail Linux flaw (CVE-2026-31431) allowing root access, with a public exploit released before patches were ready.
insight: Unpatched Linux systems remain vulnerable to root takeover through exploited kernel flaw.

title: Intel's Arc Pro B70 workstation GPU with 32GB of VRAM gets tested in games Roughly twice as fast as Arc B580 on average, beats RTX 5060 Ti in some titles
summary: Intel's Arc Pro B70 workstation GPU performs well in games, averaging 2.9% behind the RTX 5060 Ti.
insight: Arc Pro B70 closes performance gap in high-end gaming market.

title: Nvidia's exposure to Asian supply chains for components hits 90% of its production costs marked increase from 65% could intensify as physical AI adds even more exposure
summary: Nvidia's dependence on Asian suppliers has increased to 90%, up from 65%. This shift in supply chain exposes Nvidia to potential global disruptions. The trend may worsen as the AI industry grows.
insight: Nvidia's Asian supply chain exposure intensifies global vulnerability risks.

title: AMD Ryzen AI Max+ PRO 495 APU could arrive with 192GB of unified memory leaked PassMark benchmarks suggest modest update over Strix Halo
summary: The AMD Ryzen AI Max+ PRO 495 APU gains unified memory, but retains core configuration. It offers slightly better performance compared to its predecessor, the Strix Halo. The update is considered modest.
insight: Incremental performance boost with increased unified memory capacity.

title: Renesas RZ/V2H Robotics Development Kit handles AI vision, motor control, and power management with a single board
summary: The Renesas RZ/V2H Robotics Development Kit is a single-board solution for AI vision, motor control, and power management, featuring a 80 TOPS AI accelerator and a 40-pin Raspberry Pi GPIO header.
insight: AI-powered robotics development becomes more accessible and feasible on a single board.

title: Congatec conga-TC300 COM Express module features up to Intel Core 7 350 Wildcat Lake processor
summary: The congatec conga-TC300 is an entry-level edge AI module featuring Intel Wildcat Lake SoCs, up to the Core 7 350 processor, with support for up to 64GB DDR5 memory and various I/O interfaces.
insight: Enables efficient edge AI computing with high-performance Intel processors.

title: AIVON Offers $60 New User Credits for PCB Prototyping Starting from $1 (Sponsored)
summary: AIVON offers $60 in credits for new users to reduce prototyping costs. New customers receive $30 manufacturing credit and $30 shipping credit. This allows for free shipping on standard PCB prototype orders.
insight: Low-cost prototyping enables faster development and reduced financial risk.

title: ESP32-P4 + ESP32-C5 board features Raspberry Pi-compatible MIPI connectors for official displays and camera modules
summary: The ESP32-P4 + ESP32-C5 board features a compact design with four MIPI connectors, including two compatible with Raspberry Pi camera modules and displays. It contains an ESP32-P4 MCU with 32MB PSRAM, an ESP32-C5 dual-band WiFi 6 SoC, and a 16MB NOR flash. The board also includes various ports and connectors for expansion and peripherals.
insight: Enhanced display and camera integration for IoT projects.

title: Enabling Autonomous Smart Fabs: How Sensor-Driven Intelligence Is Redefining Semiconductor Manufacturing
summary: The future of semiconductor manufacturing relies on sensor-driven intelligence and AI-driven analytics to increase productivity and efficiency.
insight: Precision control enables defect-free, high-yield semiconductor production cycles consistently.

title: Artemis II update: Launch, TLI burn, toilet fix to Moon or bust
summary: Artemis II mission is underway with a Moon flyby expected Monday. The spacecraft will conduct a Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn and a toilet fix to ensure the crew's comfort during the mission. This is a crucial step towards the first crewed mission to the Moon.
insight: Pioneering a safe, habitable lunar presence for human exploration.

title: The Best CPUs: Gaming, Productivity, and the Best Bang for Your Buck
summary: Intel's resurgence and AMD's X3D dominance make the CPU market highly competitive, offering genuine value at every price point.
insight: Competitive CPU market offers better value and performance options.

title: Kids are using fake mustaches, VPNs, and their parents' accounts to get around age verification
summary: A recent survey shows UK's Online Safety Act has limited effectiveness in preventing minors from accessing social media and adult content, with some kids using tactics like fake mustaches, VPNs, and their parents' accounts to bypass age verification.
insight: Minors exploit loopholes in age verification systems with creative tactics

title: A government used AI to write its AI regulations. It did not go well
summary: A government used AI to draft an AI regulation bill, but it contained fabricated sources, sparking concerns. The incident raises questions about the reliability and accountability of AI-generated content. It's unclear what the implications will be for regulation.
insight: Unreliable AI-generated content can undermine trust in critical decision-making processes.

title: Bazzite 44 brings kernel, desktop, and security updates
summary: Bazzite 44 brings significant updates to its gaming-focused Linux distro, including improved GPU support and strengthened security.
insight: Enhanced gaming experience with improved GPU support and security features.

title: This $88 retro gaming handheld has a screen that rotates on a hinge, because why not
summary: The retro gaming handheld features an octa-core CPU, Mali G52 GPU, and 3GB RAM, while its 32GB eMCP storage is expandable via a TF card slot. It also includes WiFi 5 and Bluetooth 5.0 connectivity. The handheld's unique aspect is its screen that rotates on a hinge.
insight: Retro gaming handheld innovation boosts flexibility and user experience

title: The chip industry is booming again, but only for companies building AI infrastructure
summary: The silicon wafer industry is experiencing a 13.1% growth in shipments, driven by increased demand for AI infrastructure.
insight: AI infrastructure drives silicon wafer industry growth again.

title: 3DMark tests CPU and GPU performance with modern graphics workloads
summary: 3DMark is a benchmarking tool that measures GPU and CPU performance, useful for system checks and performance comparisons.
insight: Precise performance evaluations for modern graphics workloads and hardware configurations.

title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo mini PC is coming in June with 128GB of unified memory and a focus on local AI workloads
summary: AMD is launching a Ryzen AI Halo mini PC in June, featuring 128GB unified memory. The device focuses on local AI workloads. Pricing details are not yet available.
insight: Accelerating local AI workloads with high-performance unified memory capabilities.

title: Trivia: What was the population of the Death Star?
summary: The Death Star was a massive space station with a significant workforce. Its population is estimated to be around 1,000,000 stormtroopers, technicians, and other personnel. This enormous workforce was necessary to maintain and operate the Death Star.
insight: Massive workforce required to maintain and operate the Death Star.

title: Next-gen MRDIMM standard nears completion targeting 12,800 MT/s DDR5 transfer rates for AI and data center workloads
summary: JEDEC's updated timeline brings near-completion for the next-gen MRDIMM standard, enabling 12,800 MT/s DDR5 transfer rates for AI and data center workloads.
insight: Future-proofing data centers with significantly faster memory transfer rates.

title: The Oscars just declared that AI actors and AI-written scripts can't win awards
summary: The Oscars have clarified rules to exclude AI-created content from eligibility. Acting roles must be performed by humans with their consent, while screenplays must be human-authored. This move aims to maintain the integrity of the awards.
insight: Preserving human creative agency in the face of AI advancements

title: That time when a Waymo robotaxi drove off with a passenger's luggage after the trunk failed to unlock at the airport
summary: A Waymo robotaxi drove away with a passenger's luggage at the airport, as the trunk failed to unlock.
insight: Autonomous vehicles may struggle with basic functions like trunk operation.

title: Utah just passed the first US law targeting VPN use for age verification
summary: Utah's law requires age verification for online content, disregarding VPN use. The law considers a user to be accessing a website from Utah if they are physically located there, regardless of VPN or proxy use. This law takes effect on May 6.
insight: Challenges to online anonymity and privacy in the digital age

title: Meta says New Mexico lawsuit could force Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp out of the state
summary: A New Mexico jury found Meta liable for platform safety lies, awarding $375 million in damages. Meta may be forced out of New Mexico due to the lawsuit. The outcome could impact Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp operations.
insight: Meta's reputation and operations face significant threats in New Mexico.

title: A Chinese court just ruled companies can't fire workers simply because AI can replace them
summary: A Chinese court ruled that AI-based layoffs are not justifiable without evidence.
insight: Protecting workers' rights in the face of AI automation

title: This new material could make carbon capture dramatically cheaper by cutting the energy needed to run it
summary: Researchers at Chiba University in Japan are developing a new material called viciazites to reduce energy needs for carbon capture.
insight: Lowering energy needs makes carbon capture more affordable and efficient.
